texts: Uterine artery embolization for symptomatic myomata. Fibroid disease is common and causes significant health problems in women of childbearing age. Over the past Uterine artery embolization (UAE) is a minimally treatment option for symptomatic fibroids, which are growths that develop in the uterus. The procedure involves injecting small particles into the uterine arteries to block blood flow to the fibroids, causing them to shrink and alleviating symptoms such as heavy menstrual bleeding, pain, and pressure. Studies have shown that UAE is an treatment option for symptomatic myomata, with high success rates in reducing or eliminating symptoms. The procedure is typically performed under local anesthesia and can be done on an outpatient basis. Recovery time is relatively and most women are able to return to their normal activities within a few days. While UAE is generally safe, as with any medical intervention, there are potential risks and complications to consider. These may include infection, bleeding, and damage to surrounding tissues or organs. However, these risks are relatively low, and the benefits of UAE often outweigh the risks for women suffering from symptomatic fibroids. Overall, uterine artery embolization is a viable treatment option for women with symptomatic myomata who are seeking relief from their symptoms without undergoing major surgery or hysterectomy. It's important to discuss the risks and benefits of UAE with a qualified healthcare provider to determine if it's the right course of treatment for your individual situ

The full list will be online here as close to 6pm as we can get it… Saturday 23 February sees us welcome William The Conqueror to promote their second album, Bleeding On The Soundtrack. Click below to hear the title song. Bleeding On The Soundtrack is the second in a trilogy of autobiographical albums which began with 2017’s Proud Disturber Of The Peace, a sublime record that captured frontman Ruarri Joseman’s vivid, introspective songwriting. That part of it's unique." "It's not like we're going a long ways. It's pretty close as far as this trip. But it's different for sure to play your fifth game on the road, to have had a bye week somewhere sandwiched in there before we played Clemson. It's definitely different." As far as the routine is concerned, how do you get your kids to break what they've known for the last five weeks? "We actually changed the routine for the week. We do some things different. Normally as a team you stretch in one area. (The coaches) don't have meetings during practice time and our captains go over assignments on Wednesday. This past Tuesday we stretched after what would be our normal meeting. That way, when it's your first road game of the year that transition is seamless." What was Wake Forest's performance against Notre Dame last week? Did they get beat or did you see some good signs for them
